112.reason: [Error: When encountering a single '&', the code sets a to "ERROR" and returns, but it does not advance curPos. This means the '&' character is not consumed, and on the next call to getSym, the same '&' will be processed again, leading to an infinite loop or repeated error reporting. The curPos should be incremented to skip the '&' before returning.]

125.reason: [When encountering a single '&' or '|', the function returns "ERROR", but it does not advance curPos past the invalid character. This causes an infinite loop in parseLine because curPos is only incremented by 1 after detecting "ERROR", but the invalid symbol remains unprocessed if it's at the end of the line or followed by non-whitespace. Additionally, the error handling assumes only one error per file, but the loop may not terminate correctly.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.5 , reason:  [When encountering a single '&' or '|', the function returns "ERROR", but it does not consume the character that caused the error. This causes the same character to be reprocessed in the next call to getSym(), leading to an infinite loop or incorrect error reporting. Additionally, the error handling in parseLine increments curPos by only 1 after detecting "ERROR", but getSym has not advanced curPos past the erroneous character, so the lexer may misalign.]

262.reason: [After detecting an ERROR (e.g., single '&'), curPos is only incremented by 1 here, but the getSym function did not consume the invalid character—it returned immediately upon seeing a lone '&' or '|'. Thus, the same character will be reprocessed in the next loop iteration, causing an infinite loop or incorrect error reporting. The correct approach is to consume the invalid character inside getSym before returning "ERROR".]
